stories.title: U.S. negotiators prepare for more peace talks as Trump repeats threats to Iran
stories.description: After Iran again closed the Strait of Hormuz, President Trump said the U.S. is returning to Pakistan for negotiations, but threatened to strike Iranian infrastructure if a deal isn't reached soon.

stories.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
stories.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: The Israeli town on the frontline with Hezbollah
stories.description: BBC foreign correspondent Nick Beake visits Metula, an Israeli town surrounded on three sides by Lebanon.

stories.title: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.title: 'Ceasefire in the Middle East, especially in Lebanon, are meant to be broken', analyst says
stories.description: In the midst of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, new rounds of talks are due to take place in Washington to determine whether the truce can be extended. However, for Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, ceasefires in t...

stories.title: Iran asserts leverage in Hormuz ahead of new round of talks
stories.description: Iran has partially reopened the Strait of Hormuz, but only under strict authorisation and designated routes, as tensions with the US continue. While Pakistan tries to organise new talks, Iranian officials insist sanctions must be lifted and reject US demands to end nuclear activi...

stories.title: Israel's double game between negotiations with Lebanon and occupation in the south
stories.description: A ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon was reached following a first round of talks in Washington. Hussein Ibish, Senior Resident Scholar at the Arab Gulf States Institute in Washington, analyses Israel’s strategy in Lebanon, which he describes as oscillating between ne...

stories.title: Food security under threat as Middle East war disrupts fertiliser supply
stories.description: Around one-third of global fertiliser production comes from the Gulf region, making the area’s stability crucial for global food supply chains. With the Strait of Hormuz, an essential transit route for energy and commodities, facing repeated disruptions, concerns are growing over...

stories.title: A petition called 'Lebanon will not be the next Gaza' calls on French leaders to take action
stories.description: As France's President Emmanuel Macron is seen as a key guarantor of the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, civil society actors have launched a petition titled 'Lebanon will not be the next Gaza', calling on him to act. The initiative has been signed by numerous French public ...

stories.title: Iran: Prolonged conflict reshapes view of the regime, analyst says
stories.description: FRANCE 24’s guest Mehran Kamrave, Director of the Iranian Studies Unit at the Arab Center for Research and Policy Studies, analyses Iran’s strategy towards the United States, highlighting three key levers the regime can rely on: sanctions, the nuclear programme, and control over ...

stories.title: Middle East: Hezbollah blamed after French peacekeeper killed in Lebanon
stories.description: A French peacekeeper serving with United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon was killed and three others wounded in an ambush in southern Lebanon, with Emmanuel Macron blaming Hezbollah, which denies responsibility.

stories.title: What is UNIFIL? the UN mission in southern Lebanon explained
stories.description: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) has patrolled southern Lebanon along the border with Israel since 1978, acting as a buffer force following Israel’s invasion of the region. Expanded after the 2006 war between Israel and Hezbollah, the mission now monitors the ...

stories.title: What to know about US-Iran standoff over the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Trump accuses Iran of violating the ceasefire as he announces plans to send US negotiators to Pakistan for more talks.

stories.title: Displaced Lebanese woman returns to find home destroyed after ceasefire
stories.description: A displaced woman returned to southern Lebanon after a ceasefire to find her house reduced to rubble.

stories.title: Trump says US negotiators to head to Pakistan for Iran talks
stories.description: US president announces talks in Islamabad and accuses Iran of violating truce, warning Tehran of severe repercussions.

stories.title: Iran war: 10 frequently used words and their meanings
stories.description: The US-Israel war on Iran nears two months. We break down some of the frequently used terminology in this war.

stories.title: Iran warns Hormuz will stay shut unless US lifts siege on its ports
stories.description: Tehran is threatening to keep the Strait of Hormuz shut unless Washington lifts the ‘US siege’ on Iranian ports.

stories.title: Does Israel’s ‘Yellow Line’ violate the Lebanon ceasefire?
stories.description: The Israeli 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon raises fears that a temporary military zone could become a longer-term occupation.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 51 of the US-Israeli conflict?
stories.description: Tehran says it will keep the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed until Washington ends its blockade of Iranian ports.

stories.title: How the Israeli military targets the people who save lives
stories.description: Israeli attacks have killed at least 57 medical workers in Lebanon since early March. It is a pattern lifted from Gaza.

stories.title: Trump says US representatives heading to Islamabad, will be ‘there tomorrow evening for negotiations’
stories.description: Islamabad appeared all set on Sunday to host a second round of talks between the US and Iran, with President Donald Trump announcing that representatives from Washington were heading to Islamabad and they would be “there tomorrow evening for negotiations”. stories.title: Security beefed up as Islamabad braces for arrival of foreign delegations
stories.description: Islamabad’s Red Zone was closed for traffic as the capital braces to welcome foreign delegations, police said on Sunday, with alternate traffic plans issued to facilitate citizens. “Due to the arrival of foreign delegations in Islamabad, Red Zone and Extended Red Zone will remain...

stories.title: 17 immigrants have died in US custody this year: ICE
stories.description: WASHINGTON: At least 17 immigrants have died in US Immigration and Customs Enforcement custody from January 2026 through early April, according to ICE. That follows 31 deaths last year, a two-decade high. Aled Damien Carbonell-Betan­court of Cuba died on April 12 at a federal det...

stories.title: France blames Hezbollah for soldier’s death in Lebanon
stories.description: • Hezbollah denies allegation, calls for ‘caution in making judgements’• Macron urges Beirut to ‘guarantee security’ of UNIFIL peacekeepers PARIS / BEIRUT: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack on Saturday on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon that appeared to...

stories.title: Mass rallies in Germany demand faster shift to renewable energy
stories.description: BERLIN: Thousands of people demonstrated acr­oss Germany on Saturday, urging a faster shift to re­­newable energy and accusing conservative Chancel­lor Friedrich Merz’s coalition of putting the brakes on the transition. In Berlin, Cologne, Hamburg and Munich, crowds took to the s...

stories.title: Pope Leo downplays feud with Trump, says ‘not in my interest’ to debate him
stories.description: Pope Leo sought to downplay his feud with US President Donald Trump on Saturday, saying reporting about comments he has made so far during his Africa tour “has not been accurate in all its aspects”. Speaking to reporters in English aboard his flight to Angola for the third leg of...

stories.title: Donald Trump says U.S. officials will be in Pakistan ‘for negotiations’
stories.description: The U.S. President reiterated that “We’re offering a very fair and reasonable DEAL, and I hope they take it because, if they don’t, the United States is going to knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran.”

stories.title: Iran-Israel war LIVE: Donald Trump says U.S. officials will be in Pakistan ‘for negotiations’
stories.description: Security in Pakistan's capital Islamabad was stepped up visibly ahead of peace talks between the United States and Iran

stories.title: Pakistan tightens security in Islamabad ahead of U.S.-Iran talks
stories.description: President Donald Trump said U.S. negotiators will be in Pakistan on Monday (April 21, 2026) to resume talks on ending the war with Iran

stories.title: Iran to resume international flights from Mashhad airport on April 20
stories.description: Iranian airports have been closed since the outbreak of war with Israel and the United States on February 28

stories.title: Iran says final deal still far off as Hormuz Strait shuttered
stories.description: On April 22, Tehran declared the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas usually transits, open after a temporary ceasefire was agreed to halt Israel’s war with Iran’s ally Hezbollah in Lebanon

stories.title: Iran says final deal still far off as Strait of Hormuz remains closed
stories.description: A two-week ceasefire is set to end on Wednesday (April 22, 2026) unless it is renewed

stories.title: US diplomat says attacks on Iranian power plants will not be war crime
stories.description: The Iranian air defenses have been absolutely decimated, US Permanent Representative to the United Nations Mike Waltz said

stories.title: Iran to decide on talks with US only after naval blockade is lifted — agency
stories.description: According to Tasnim, contacts between Iran and the United States have continues in recent days via Pakistani mediators

stories.title: Trump threatens to destroy all power plants, bridges in Iran if it rejects deal
stories.description: The US offers "a very fair and reasonable deal", the American leader said

stories.title: Deal with Iran could be expected within two weeks — US energy secretary
stories.description: Asked if Vice President JD Vance is going to travel to Islamabad on Monday for a new round of talks with Iran, Chris Wright said that "the vice president's been leading the negotiations from the start"
